name: Wilson Pickett
abstract: Wilson Pickett (March 18, 1941 – January 19, 2006) was an American singer and songwriter. A major figure in the development of soul music, Pickett recorded more than 50 songs that made the US R&B charts, many of which crossed over to the Billboard Hot 100.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: We’d do these shows with Stevie Wonder and Gladys Knight & the Pips and Wilson Pickett, Al Green, all of these really heavy soul acts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: Wilson Pickett was the best-known artist is Ghana of all of the people that were there... Through that whole tour, Wilson Pickett was the main man there.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: Because they had Aretha, they had Wilson Pickett, they had the whole Memphis thing with Otis and Sam & Dave and you know, that crew.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: You know like Wilson Pickett and those people in the beginning, I just loved the rhythm feel you know in those records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: Wilson Pickett or somebody like that was supposed to be on the band and he didn't get there. He was late.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
